claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) reflects a label-encoding regime in which most tokens are drawn from a restricted lexicon of fewer than 80 unique word types, producing a word-frequency distribution with Zipf exponent significantly steeper (greater than 1.2) than the full corpus (0.8946), consistent with a closed enumeration rather than free prose.
evidence_for: Zodiac section has lowest entropy (3.7149 bits) suggesting reduced glyph diversity consistent with label repetition
Zodiac has only 1,297 words across 12 folios â€” low density consistent with label-heavy layout
Existing evidence (0.711, 0.723) supports zodiac as label-oriented encoding
evidence_against: Low entropy could alternatively reflect a denser homophonic substitution rather than label restriction
If zodiac labels encode planet/month names in an unknown cipher, the lexicon size may still be large
Pharmaceutical section also has low entropy (3.7772) but is not typically described as label-heavy, weakening entropy as a unique diagnostic
test: Extract the zodiac section token list; compute unique word count, Zipf exponent on zodiac word frequencies alone, and coverage of the top-80 most frequent zodiac word types as a fraction of all zodiac tokens; compare Zipf exponent to full-corpus exponent using a permutation test.
test_metric: Unique word count in zodiac section; Zipf exponent for zodiac word-frequency distribution; percentage of zodiac tokens covered by top-80 word types
test_threshold: Confirm label-encoding if unique word count is below 80 distinct types and top-80 coverage exceeds 95% of all zodiac tokens and Zipf exponent exceeds 1.2; deny if unique word count exceeds 150 or top-80 coverage is below 80%

tests_run.test: extract the zodiac section token list; compute unique word count, zipf exponent on zodiac word frequ
tests_run.details: Observed Zipf exponent: 0.8946 (R²=0.9084). Natural language expected: ~1.0. Fit quality: moderate.

reasoning: A closed label lexicon would sharply distinguish the zodiac section from prose sections and would rule out free-text cipher models for that section, allowing section-specific decipherment strategies to be applied.
